content.title: Funds Release Dogfood 2
content.description: Controlled release of funds held in a blocked or pending state once verification, approval, and compliance conditions are satisfied, covering both manual and straight-through (STP) paths against approved corporate credit facilities.
content.trigger: A drawdown request received from the front office for an approved facility; or a held payment reaching its scheduled release date. Rarely (fewer than 10 % of volume): an Operations Analyst manually initiates the release of a previously flagged or held item.
content.frequency: 15–25 per business day, with peaks around month-end
content.scopeIn: Release of funds against an approved corporate credit facility; both manual and straight-through (STP) processing paths.
content.scopeOut: Origination or approval of the underlying facility; retail customer payments; FX conversion and correspondent-bank / SWIFT settlement legs. The domestic Core Banking posting in the Execute Release step is in scope.
content.processInput: Release request carrying facility ID, amount, currency, value date, and beneficiary details.
content.processOutput: Posted funds transfer to the beneficiary instruction and confirmation sent to the front office, with the workflow item closed and the audit log updated.

Purpose.evidence: Payments Workflow Tool — queue, routing, approvals and audit log.

Role in this process.evidence: SME confirmed three additions: (1) step titles in place of ps-X shorthands; (2) system-enforces 4-eyes SoD per CP-FRDT-003; (3) queue has no priority ordering, documenting current state per PP-FRDT-002.

content.title: Payments Workflow Tool
content.purpose: Manages the payment operations queue, including item routing, approvals and audit log.
content.roleInThisProcess: Primary orchestration layer: receives requests (Receive Request, PS-FRDT-007), routes through First-Line and Second-Line Approval (PS-FRDT-003, PS-FRDT-005), closes with a timestamped audit record (Confirm and Close, PS-FRDT-006). System-enforces 4-eyes SoD (CP-FRDT-003). Queue has no priority ordering (PP-FRDT-002).

Role in this process.evidence: SME confirmed two-leg posting at Execute Release (debit held/facility, credit beneficiary/nostro); Confirm and Close reads posting confirmation from CBS (read only); Payments Workflow Tool holds workflow-closure record.

content.title: Core Banking System
content.purpose: Executes account postings and fund movement.
content.roleInThisProcess: Posts both legs at Execute Release (PS-FRDT-008): debit held/facility account, credit beneficiary/nostro. Confirm and Close (PS-FRDT-006) reads the posting confirmation back from the CBS; the Payments Workflow Tool holds the workflow-closure record.

Role in this process.evidence: SME confirmed: clean items pass automatically; potential hits flagged and routed to Compliance, blocking release until resolved. SAR-filing and formal investigation are Compliance actions in a separate case-management/AML system, not attributable to this engine.

content.title: Sanctions Screening Engine
content.purpose: Provides real-time sanctions and AML screening.
content.roleInThisProcess: Invoked at the Compliance Screening step (PS-FRDT-002): clean items pass automatically, while potential hits are flagged and routed to Compliance for adjudication, blocking release until the investigation is resolved.

Role in this process.evidence: SME confirmed: manual interaction — Treasury Analyst checks the platform and manually records funding confirmation in the Payments Workflow Tool; no automated API query or response. Explains the absent SLA documented in PP-FRDT-002.

content.title: Treasury / Liquidity Platform
content.purpose: Provides funding availability confirmation for Treasury operations.
content.roleInThisProcess: Used at the Liquidity Confirmation step (PS-FRDT-004) for releases at or above EUR 5,000,000: a Treasury Analyst checks the platform and manually records the funding confirmation in the Payments Workflow Tool — no automated API query or response.

Role in this process.evidence: SME confirmed: FMS consulted (read only) at Validate Request; limit check is CP-FRDT-001. FMS update on Execute Release (drawn amount decrementing available limit) is undocumented — whether automated or manual is a gap deferred to the IT-architect pass.

content.title: Facility Management System
content.purpose: Maintains the register of corporate credit facilities, including their status and available undrawn limit.
content.roleInThisProcess: Consulted at the Validate Request step (PS-FRDT-001): the Operations Analyst verifies that the facility ID exists, is in Active status, and that the requested amount does not exceed the available limit — a prerequisite for CP-FRDT-001 (Facility Limit Check).

What it requires.evidence: SME named instruments: Reg. 2580/2001, EU Consolidated Sanctions List, 6AMLD as nationally transposed, EU Funds Transfer Regulation (2015/847). OFAC SDN for USD-leg. Obligation persists regardless of specific instrument.

Why it applies.evidence: SME broadened: obligation applies to every release the process executes, not only credit-facility drawdowns.

How it is met.evidence: SME confirmed accurate; C-2 corrected to CP-FRDT-002. SAR and formal investigation added per earlier CP-FRDT-002 confirmation.

content.title: AML and Sanctions Obligations
content.whatItRequires: EU sanctions regulations (Regulation 2580/2001, EU Consolidated Sanctions List), 6AMLD as nationally transposed, and EU Funds Transfer Regulation (2015/847) impose screening and payer/payee information obligations before release. OFAC SDN screening applies to USD-leg releases. The obligation persists regardless of which specific instrument is in force.
content.whyItApplies: The process executes payment releases in all forms. The screening obligation applies to every release, not only credit-facility drawdowns — the bank must screen all outbound fund movements before execution.
content.howItIsMet: Met via the Compliance Screening step: every item is automatically screened before release; confirmed hits freeze the item and trigger Compliance to file a SAR and open a formal investigation (CP-FRDT-002).
content.source: Regulatory context section: 'anti-money-laundering and sanctions obligations (EU sanctions regulations, AML directives)'

What it requires.evidence: SME confirmed high-level framing is accurate; field-level detail (2015/847 data fields, intra-EU vs third-country distinctions) belongs in control-compliance specialist pass.

content.title: EU Funds Transfer Regulation
content.whatItRequires: The EU Funds Transfer Regulation requires that payer and payee information accompanies each payment instruction.
content.whyItApplies: Each funds-release instruction is an outbound payment; missing or incomplete beneficiary and payer details breach the regulation.
content.howItIsMet: Met by CP-FRDT-006 (Payment Data Completeness) at the Validate Request step (PS-FRDT-001), which verifies that all mandatory EU Funds Transfer Regulation payer and payee identification fields are present before the item advances to compliance screening.
content.source: Regulatory context section: 'EU Funds Transfer Regulation for payer / payee information'

What it requires.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/sites/default/files/document_library/Publications/Guidelines/2021/1016721/Final%20report%20on%20Guidelines%20on%20internal%20governance%20under%20CRD.pdf — "When implementing the internal control framework, institutions should establish adequate segregation of duties – e.g. entrusting conflicting activities within the processing of transactions or when providing services to different persons, or entrusting supervisory and reporting responsibilities for conflicting activities to different persons" (§149) — fetched 2026-06-05

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/sites/default/files/document_library/Publications/Guidelines/2021/1016721/Final%20report%20on%20Guidelines%20on%20internal%20governance%20under%20CRD.pdf — EBA/GL/2021/05 Section 16 applies to all EU credit institutions; §149 explicitly covers segregation in "processing of transactions" — fetched 2026-06-05

How it is met.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/sites/default/files/document_library/Publications/Guidelines/2021/1016721/Final%20report%20on%20Guidelines%20on%20internal%20governance%20under%20CRD.pdf — §149 requires institutions to separate conflicting activities; CP-FRDT-003 (4-eyes SoD via PWT) implements this — fetched 2026-06-05

content.title: EBA Internal Governance: Segregation of Duties in Payment Authorization
content.whatItRequires: Requires institutions to establish adequate segregation of duties — entrusting conflicting activities in the processing of transactions to different persons — as part of the internal control framework for material business processes.
content.whyItApplies: FRDT-001's approval chain requires first-line and second-line authorization by different individuals; executing both constitutes a conflicting activity within transaction processing — directly within EBA/GL/2021/05 §149's scope.
content.howItIsMet: Met by CP-FRDT-003 (Segregation of Duties), which system-enforces the 4-eyes rule via the Payments Workflow Tool, blocking the same user ID from recording both approval steps.
content.source: EBA/GL/2021/05 — Guidelines on internal governance under Directive 2013/36/EU, §149 (Title V, Section 16)

What it requires.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/sites/default/files/document_library/Publications/Guidelines/2020/Guidelines%20on%20loan%20origination%20and%20monitoring/884283/EBA%20GL%202020%2006%20Final%20Report%20on%20GL%20on%20loan%20origination%20and%20monitoring.pdf — "As part of the monitoring of credit exposures and borrowers, institutions should monitor all outstanding amounts and limits, and whether the borrower is meeting repayment obligations, as laid down in the credit agreement" (§251, Section 8.2) — fetched 2026-06-05

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/sites/default/files/document_library/Publications/Guidelines/2020/Guidelines%20on%20loan%20origination%20and%20monitoring/884283/EBA%20GL%202020%2006%20Final%20Report%20on%20GL%20on%20loan%20origination%20and%20monitoring.pdf — §251 Section 8.2 requires ongoing monitoring of credit exposures; each FRDT-001 drawdown is a utilization event against an outstanding credit facility — fetched 2026-06-05

How it is met.evidence: https://www.eba.europa.eu/sites/default/files/document_library/Publications/Guidelines/2020/Guidelines%20on%20loan%20origination%20and%20monitoring/884283/EBA%20GL%202020%2006%20Final%20Report%20on%20GL%20on%20loan%20origination%20and%20monitoring.pdf — §251 monitoring obligation; CP-FRDT-001 performs the facility limit check at PS-FRDT-001 — fetched 2026-06-05

content.title: EBA Loan Origination and Monitoring: Credit Facility Utilization Controls
content.whatItRequires: Requires institutions to monitor all outstanding credit facility amounts and limits, and whether borrowers are meeting credit agreement conditions — including adherence to credit metrics and covenants — as part of the ongoing credit exposure monitoring framework.
content.whyItApplies: Each FRDT-001 drawdown request is a utilization event against an outstanding credit facility; the facility limit check is the operational implementation of EBA/GL/2020/06's ongoing monitoring obligation for credit exposures.
content.howItIsMet: Met by CP-FRDT-001 (Facility Limit Check) at the Validate Request step (PS-FRDT-001), which verifies the requested amount against the approved facility limit and available undrawn balance before the request progresses.
content.source: EBA/GL/2020/06 — Guidelines on loan origination and monitoring, §251 (Section 8.2 — Monitoring of credit exposures and borrowers)

meta.source: BCBS 248 / ECB Sound Practices for Managing Intraday Liquidity Risk (2024)

What it requires.evidence: https://www.bis.org/publ/bcbs248.pdf — "A bank should actively manage its intraday liquidity positions and risks to meet payment and settlement obligations on a timely basis under both normal and stressed conditions" (BCBS 248, Principle 8 / para. 1) — fetched 2026-06-05

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.bankingsupervision.europa.eu/ecb/pub/pdf/ssm.Sound_practices_for_managing_intraday_liquidity_risk_202411~aec6d34ffa.en.pdf — ECB Sound Practices (Nov 2024) Key Practice 2: "The institution's Treasury function either monitors intraday developments actively or stands ready to step in when the system issues an alert" — directly describes the manual Treasury confirmation model in PS-FRDT-004 — fetched 2026-06-05

How it is met.evidence: https://www.bis.org/publ/bcbs248.pdf — BCBS 248 para. 2(ii) requires capacity to monitor intraday positions against available resources; CP-FRDT-004 is the current compensating manual control pending a real-time Treasury API — fetched 2026-06-05

content.title: BCBS 248: Intraday Liquidity Monitoring for Payment Settlement
content.whatItRequires: Requires banks to actively manage intraday liquidity positions and risks to meet payment and settlement obligations on time — including capacity to monitor intraday positions against available resources and arrange sufficient intraday funding across the business day.
content.whyItApplies: FRDT-001 executes large-value drawdown payments that consume intraday liquidity; the Treasury funding confirmation for drawdowns ≥EUR 5m is the process-level operationalization of the BCBS 248 intraday monitoring obligation.
content.howItIsMet: Met by CP-FRDT-004 (Treasury Funding Confirmation) at PS-FRDT-004; current implementation is manual. ECB Sound Practices (Nov 2024) and BCBS 248 direction of travel is toward real-time automated monitoring; the manual gap is documented in PP-FRDT-002.
content.source: BCBS 248 — Monitoring tools for intraday liquidity management (April 2013); ECB Banking Supervision — Sound practices for managing intraday liquidity risk (November 2024)

What it requires.evidence: https://www.bafin.de/SharedDocs/Downloads/EN/Rundschreiben/rs_1021_marisk_ba_en.pdf?__blob=publicationFile&v=3 — AT 4.3.1: "When determining the organisational and operational structure, the institution must ensure that incompatible activities are properly separated from one another. This applies in particular to material business processes and the related control activities." BTO 1.2.1: lending decisions require separation of market-area and back-office functions up to management level; disbursement may only be effected once all preconditions set at credit approval have been satisfied — fetched 2026-06-05

Why it applies.evidence: https://www.bafin.de/SharedDocs/Downloads/EN/Rundschreiben/rs_1021_marisk_ba_en.pdf?__blob=publicationFile&v=3 — MaRisk applies to all German-supervised institutions; FRDT-001 is a material payment process with a credit-disbursement dimension; AT 4.3.1 and BTO 1.2.1 apply to the approval chain and drawdown preconditions — fetched 2026-06-05

How it is met.evidence: https://www.bafin.de/SharedDocs/Downloads/EN/Rundschreiben/rs_1021_marisk_ba_en.pdf?__blob=publicationFile&v=3 — AT 4.3.1 SoD met by CP-FRDT-003; BTO 1.2.1 credit preconditions met by CP-FRDT-001 (facility limit) and PS-FRDT-001 (Validate Request); CP-FRDT-005 (Daily Reconciliation) supports AT 4.3 internal control reporting — fetched 2026-06-05

content.title: MaRisk AT 4.3 / BTO 1.2: Operational Governance and Credit Disbursement Authorization
content.whatItRequires: AT 4.3.1 requires incompatible activities to be properly separated in material business processes and related controls. BTO 1.2.1 requires lending decisions to follow market-area / back-office separation up to management level; disbursement may only occur once all preconditions set at credit approval have been satisfied.
content.whyItApplies: FRDT-001 is a material payment process with a credit-disbursement dimension; if the institution is BaFin-supervised, MaRisk AT 4.3.1 governs the SoD in the approval chain and BTO 1.2.1 governs the drawdown preconditions that must be satisfied before execution.
content.howItIsMet: AT 4.3.1 met by CP-FRDT-003 (4-eyes SoD); BTO 1.2.1 credit preconditions met by CP-FRDT-001 (facility limit) and PS-FRDT-001 (Validate Request); CP-FRDT-005 (Daily Reconciliation) supports AT 4.3's internal control reporting obligations.
content.source: BaFin Circular 10/2021 — Minimum Requirements for Risk Management (MaRisk), AT 4.3.1 / BTO 1.2.1

What it requires.evidence: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32015L2366 — Article 83(1): Member States shall require the payer's PSP to ensure the amount is credited to the payee's PSP account by end of the following business day — fetched 2026-06-05

Why it applies.evidence: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/HTML/?uri=CELEX:32015L2366 — Art. 83 not in Art. 61 derogable list; Art. 2(3) confirms it applies to one-leg transactions — fetched 2026-06-05

content.title: PSD2 Article 83: Payment Execution Timelines
content.whatItRequires: Requires the payer's PSP to credit the payee's PSP account by end of the following business day after time of receipt — a D+1 statutory maximum for all payment transactions.
content.whyItApplies: Each FRDT-001 release is an outbound payment transaction; PSD2 Art. 83 sets the statutory execution ceiling and is not derogable for corporate clients under Art. 61.
content.howItIsMet: The execution chain (PS-FRDT-007 through PS-FRDT-008) and the 14:00 CET cut-off operationalise D+1 compliance. No dedicated SLA-monitoring control exists; this exposure is formally recorded in CG-FRDT-001 (PSD2 Execution Timeline — No Dedicated Monitoring Control).
content.source: Directive (EU) 2015/2366 on Payment Services (PSD2), Article 83

What happens.evidence: Signed drawdown notice and facility utilisation request are the required docs; resubmitted items retain original workflow ID and re-enter at Validate Request (confirmed by Markus Holzhäuser)

Inputs.evidence: Required docs for a facility drawdown are the signed drawdown notice and facility utilisation request (confirmed by Markus Holzhäuser)

Outputs.evidence: Incomplete requests are returned to the front office.

Why it matters.evidence: Incomplete requests are returned to the front office — this is the most common reason for delay. The requested amount does not exceed the available undrawn limit.

Request is incomplete — returned to front office for correction; the item retains its original workflow ID and re-enters Validate Request on resubmission (cycle time measured from original receipt, not resubmission date)

meta.description: The Operations Analyst performs a structured completeness and eligibility check on the queued release item. The analyst confirms that the facility ID exists and is in Active status, the requested amount does not exceed the available undrawn limit, the value date is a valid business day, and the signed drawdown notice and facility utilisation request are attached. Requests that fail any check are returned to the front office — this is the most common source of delay in the process.

Facility record and available limit from the Facility Management System
Business day calendar
Signed drawdown notice and facility utilisation request (attached to the workflow item)

Returned request (if incomplete) with reason noted for front office
meta.businessValue: Validation prevents defective instructions from reaching the approval and execution stages, where errors are costlier to reverse. It also enforces the facility limit control, blocking releases that would exceed the authorised credit exposure.

meta.condition: Applies to both newly queued items and reactivated items returned by the front office after correction. Reactivated items retain their original workflow ID — cycle time is measured from original receipt.

What it checks.evidence: Release is blocked unless the available facility limit covers the requested amount.

Control activity.evidence: On insufficient limit: item blocked and returned to front office via same return route as validation failure.

Risk addressed.evidence: SME reframed: credit over-exposure — drawing down more than the authorised undrawn facility limit.

Timing.evidence: The Operations Analyst checks: requested amount does not exceed the available undrawn limit (step 2).

content.title: Facility Limit Check
content.whatItChecks: Verifies that the available undrawn facility limit is sufficient to cover the requested release amount before the item is allowed to proceed.
content.controlActivity: Automatically blocks release unless the available facility limit covers the requested amount. On an insufficient limit, the item is blocked and returned to the front office via the same return route as a validation failure.
content.riskAddressed: Credit over-exposure: drawing down more than the authorised undrawn facility limit against an approved corporate credit facility.
content.timing: Applied at the point of request validation (PS-FRDT-001, the document's step 2) for every release request.

What it checks.evidence: Every item is screened before release.

Risk addressed.evidence: SME named regimes: EU Consolidated Sanctions List, OFAC SDN list, UN sanctions lists, money laundering; exposure is regulatory enforcement and licence or supervisory sanction.

Timing.evidence: Compliance screening step (step 3): The item is screened for sanctions and AML hits.

content.title: Sanctions and AML Screening
content.whatItChecks: Screens every item for sanctions list matches and anti-money-laundering indicators before any release is authorised.
content.controlActivity: The Sanctions Screening Engine screens each item. Clean items pass; hits go to Compliance for adjudication. A confirmed hit freezes the item — does not proceed — and triggers a SAR and formal investigation. Thresholds and matching rules are validated at least annually by Compliance: calibration, FP/FN sampling, watchlist-update confirmation.
content.riskAddressed: Release of funds to parties on the EU Consolidated Sanctions List, OFAC SDN list, or UN sanctions lists, or in furtherance of money laundering — exposing the bank to regulatory enforcement and licence or supervisory sanction.
content.timing: Runs automatically during the Compliance Screening step (step 3) for every release item, prior to any approval.

What it checks.evidence: First-line and second-line approval must be performed by different people.

Control activity.evidence: Payments Workflow Tool system-enforces the rule: blocks same user ID from recording both first-line and second-line approval; not purely procedural. Confirmed by SME.

Risk addressed.evidence: SME added regulatory dimension: dual control for payment authorisation required under EBA Guidelines on Internal Governance and bank internal-control standards; covers both fraud/error prevention and regulatory obligation.

Timing.evidence: Second-line approval (4-eyes) step (step 6): A separate Operations Approver independently reviews and authorises the release.

content.title: Segregation of Duties (4-Eyes)
content.whatItChecks: Verifies that the individual performing second-line approval is different from the individual who performed the first-line approval.
content.controlActivity: The Payments Workflow Tool system-enforces the 4-eyes rule: it blocks the same user ID from recording both first-line and second-line approval. A separate Operations Approver independently reviews and authorises the release — the system prevents self-approval regardless of role.
content.riskAddressed: Unauthorised or fraudulent release by a single individual without independent oversight, and a breach of the regulatory dual-control obligation for payment authorisation under EBA Guidelines on Internal Governance and the bank's internal-control standards.
content.timing: Applied at every second-line approval (step 6) for all release items, without exception.

What it checks.evidence: For amounts at or above the Treasury threshold (currently EUR 5,000,000 equivalent), Treasury confirms funding availability for the value date.

Control activity.evidence: Treasury confirms funding availability for the value date. If funding is unavailable the release is deferred and rescheduled to the next available value date.

Risk addressed.evidence: SME confirmed: settlement shortfall or intraday liquidity exposure is the right level; no need to name prudential ratios. Deferral-to-next-value-date path confirmed accurate.

Timing.evidence: Liquidity confirmation step (step 5): For amounts at or above the Treasury threshold (currently EUR 5,000,000 equivalent), Treasury confirms funding availability for the value date.

content.title: Treasury Funding Confirmation
content.whatItChecks: Confirms that funding is available for the value date before a large-value release is authorised, specifically for amounts at or above EUR 5,000,000 equivalent.
content.controlActivity: Treasury confirms funding availability for the value date. If funding is unavailable, the release is deferred and rescheduled to the next available value date.
content.riskAddressed: Execution of a large-value release without confirmed funding, creating a settlement shortfall or intraday liquidity breach.
content.timing: Performed during the Liquidity Confirmation step (step 5) for every release request at or above the EUR 5,000,000 threshold.

What it checks.evidence: Daily reconciliation of held vs. released balances.

Control activity.evidence: SME confirmed: discrepancy investigated, root-caused and corrected same day; unresolved breaks escalated to Operations Team Lead.

Risk addressed.evidence: SME confirmed text; added supervisory-reporting dimension: unresolved break past end of day can produce balance misstatements flowing into regulatory and financial reporting.

Timing.evidence: Daily reconciliation of held vs. released balances.

content.title: Daily Reconciliation of Held vs. Released Balances
content.whatItChecks: Verifies that held balances and released balances reconcile with the expected positions at end of day.
content.controlActivity: Operations performs a daily manual reconciliation of held and released balance totals. Any discrepancy is investigated, root-caused and corrected the same day; unresolved breaks are escalated to the Operations Team Lead.
content.riskAddressed: Undetected posting errors, missed releases, or duplicate postings resulting in balance discrepancies going unresolved; an unresolved break past end of day can produce balance misstatements flowing into regulatory and financial reporting.
content.timing: Performed daily, covering all items processed during the day.

What it checks.evidence: SME confirmed Y to Payment Data Completeness as the payer/payee completeness check satisfying EU Funds Transfer Regulation 2015/847 at Validate Request (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: Payment Data Completeness
content.whatItChecks: Verifies that every release instruction carries the payer and payee data fields mandated by the EU Funds Transfer Regulation — beneficiary name, account identifier, and payment reference — before advancing beyond validation.
content.controlActivity: The Operations Analyst checks for the presence and completeness of all required identification fields at the Validate Request step. An instruction missing any required field is returned to the front office with a specific reason code and cannot progress to compliance screening until the deficiency is corrected.
content.riskAddressed: Execution of a payment lacking mandatory payer and payee identification data as required by Regulation 2015/847, constituting a regulatory breach at settlement and exposing the bank to supervisory action.
content.timing: Applied at PS-FRDT-001 (Validate Request) for every release item, in parallel with the facility limit check, before compliance screening begins.

What it checks.evidence: SME requested a post-execution confirmation/reconciliation check that the posting matches the approved instruction (detective, owner Operations Analyst) at PS-FRDT-008 (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: Post-Execution Confirmation Match
content.whatItChecks: Verifies that the Core Banking System posting confirmation matches the approved instruction — same amount, beneficiary, and value date — before the workflow item is closed.
content.controlActivity: At the Confirm and Close step, the Operations Analyst reviews the Core Banking System posting confirmation against the approved instruction. Any discrepancy in posted amount, beneficiary, or value date triggers an immediate investigation before the workflow item is closed and the front-office confirmation is sent.
content.riskAddressed: Undetected discrepancy between the approved instruction and the executed posting — due to fraud, system error, or manual override — resulting in a release of the wrong amount or to the wrong beneficiary.
content.timing: Performed at PS-FRDT-006 (Confirm and Close) for every executed item, after the CBS posting confirmation is received and before the workflow item is closed.

content.title: Operations Analyst
content.responsibility: Owns intake, completeness validation, first-line approval, payment execution, and post-release confirmation for all funds-release items in the Operations queue.
content.inThisProcess: Receives requests via the Payments Workflow Tool (Receive Request), submits items for compliance screening (Compliance Screening), performs the facility-limit and document check (Validate Request), applies the first-line approval after screening results are clear (First-Line Approval), executes the posting instruction (Execute Release), and sends the front-office confirmation to close the item (Confirm and Close). Owns the Facility Limit Check control (CP-FRDT-001); first-line leg of the Segregation of Duties control (CP-FRDT-003).

Responsibility.evidence: Operations Approver — independent second-line (4-eyes) authorisation. Step 6: The approver must not be the same person who performed the first-line approval. C-3: First-line and second-line approval must be performed by different people.

In this process.evidence: Confirmed by Markus Holzhäuser — Approver reviews items and records second-line authorisation in the Payments Workflow Tool (SYS-FRDT-001)

content.title: Operations Approver
content.responsibility: Provides independent second-line authorisation, ensuring no single person both validates and approves a release.
content.inThisProcess: Independently reviews and authorises the release at Second-Line Approval (PS-FRDT-005). Must be a different individual from the first-line approver, as required by C-3 Segregation of Duties (4-Eyes).

If funding is unavailable the release is deferred and rescheduled. C-4 control owner: Treasury. Systems: Treasury / Liquidity Platform.

content.title: Treasury
content.responsibility: Confirms funding availability for high-value releases above the EUR 5,000,000 threshold.

Owns the deferral and rescheduling decision when funding is unavailable. Owns C-4 Treasury Funding Confirmation.

content.title: Compliance
content.responsibility: Adjudicates sanctions and AML alerts, determining whether flagged items may proceed or must be frozen pending investigation.
content.inThisProcess: Engaged at Compliance Screening (PS-FRDT-002) when the Sanctions Screening Engine raises a potential hit. Clean items pass without Compliance involvement; Compliance adjudicates all hits. On a confirmed hit, Compliance freezes the item and initiates SAR filing and investigation. Owns C-2 Sanctions and AML Screening.

Responsibility.evidence: Front Office — submits drawdown requests; receives confirmation.

In this process.evidence: Trigger: a drawdown request is received from the front office for an approved facility. Step 1: Each item carries a facility ID, amount, currency, value date and beneficiary details. Step 8: A confirmation is sent to the front office and the workflow item is closed. Step 2: Incomplete requests are returned to the front office — this is the most common reason for delay.

content.title: Front Office
content.responsibility: Originates drawdown requests against approved credit facilities and receives release confirmations once the funds are posted.
content.inThisProcess: Submits the drawdown request that triggers the process (ps-1), providing facility ID, amount, currency, value date, and beneficiary details. Is informed when the release is complete and the workflow item is closed (ps-8). Incomplete submissions are the most common source of processing delays.

Responsibility.evidence: Operations Team Lead — reassignment and escalation. C-5 control owner: Operations Team Lead. C-5: Daily reconciliation of held vs. released balances. Detective / manual.

In this process.evidence: Team Lead owns CP-FRDT-005 and runs daily reconciliation operationally — not delegated. Escalation triggers: item breaching processing SLA; confirmed compliance/sanctions hold; 4-eyes or segregation-of-duties conflict; same-day value-date item at risk near 14:00 CET cut-off.

content.title: Operations Team Lead
content.responsibility: Accountable for item reassignment, escalation handling, and daily reconciliation of held versus released balances.
content.inThisProcess: Handles reassignment and escalation on: SLA breach, compliance or sanctions hold, 4-eyes or segregation conflict, or a same-day value-date item at risk before 14:00 CET. Owns and operationally runs CP-FRDT-005 Daily Reconciliation of Held vs. Released Balances — confirms no items remain incorrectly held at end of day.

Definition.evidence: SME confirmed: clock starts at Operations/PWT intake (receipt into queue), stops at final posting and confirmation — correct span for an STP-turnaround SLA.

Current reading.evidence: SME confirmed: no actuals tracking exists today; 2-hour SLA target is monitored anecdotally only.

Why it matters.evidence: SME confirmed: near-immediate availability framing and capacity/bottleneck signal are accurate.

content.title: STP Release Turnaround
content.definition: The elapsed time from receipt of a clean straight-through processing release request to final posting and confirmation, measured in business hours.
content.currentReading: No measured actual is available. The SLA target is same business day, within 2 hours of receipt.
content.whyItMatters: Corporate clients expect near-immediate availability of drawn funds; a breach of the 2-hour target signals a capacity or control bottleneck.
content.target: Same business day, within 2 hours of receipt

Definition.evidence: SME confirmed: identification = when analyst logs item to exception queue (not raw PWT flag); clearance = exception resolved, whether the release then executes or is correctly frozen/cancelled (e.g. confirmed sanctions hit).

Current reading.evidence: SME confirmed: no actuals tracking; 1-business-day SLA target is document-sourced.

Why it matters.evidence: SME confirmed: delay-of-funds and exception-handling/escalation framing are accurate.

content.title: Exception Resolution Time
content.definition: The time from when the analyst logs an item to the exception queue to resolution of the exception — whether the release then executes, or is correctly frozen or cancelled (e.g. a confirmed sanctions hit).
content.currentReading: No measured actual is available. The SLA target is resolution within 1 business day of identification.
content.whyItMatters: Unresolved exceptions delay the release of funds; breaching the 1-business-day target indicates issues in exception handling and escalation paths.

Definition.evidence: SME confirmed: 14:00 CET is the internal Operations cut-off; posting must complete by then to qualify for same-day value; deliberately set earlier than the external TARGET2/SWIFT settlement cut-off to preserve a posting buffer.

Current reading.evidence: SME confirmed: document-sourced cut-off of 14:00 CET is accurate.

Why it matters.evidence: SME confirmed: deferral-to-next-value-date on missed cut-off is accurate.

content.title: Same-Day Value Cut-Off
content.definition: The internal Operations cut-off by which a release must be fully approved and posted to qualify for same-day value; set earlier than the external TARGET2/SWIFT settlement cut-off to preserve a posting buffer.
content.currentReading: No measured actual is available. The operational cut-off deadline is 14:00 CET.
content.whyItMatters: Requests that miss the 14:00 CET cut-off are deferred to the next available value date, as noted in the Treasury liquidity step and the known pain points.

Description.evidence: Items frequently stall at validation when the front office submits an incomplete request or an inactive facility ID. Required documents named as signed drawdown notice and facility utilisation request (consistent with Validate step).

Impact.evidence: Incomplete requests are returned to the front office — this is the most common reason for delay.

Root cause.evidence: SME: two causes — (1) no pre-submission validation template or field-completeness check in the FO origination tool; (2) facility status not surfaced at submission time so stale/inactive IDs pass through. The absent Operations-side gate is a missing control, not a cause.

content.title: Incomplete Front-Office Submissions
content.description: Release requests submitted by the front office frequently arrive at the Validate Request step with missing fields — most commonly an inactive or incorrect facility ID or absent required documents (signed drawdown notice or facility utilisation request) — forcing the Operations Analyst to return the item before processing can continue.
content.impact: Identified as the most common reason for delay in the funds-release process; each return extends cycle time and risks breaching same-day STP turnaround targets.
content.rootCause: No pre-submission validation template or field-completeness check exists in the front-office origination tool. Facility status is not surfaced to the FO at submission time, so stale or inactive facility IDs pass through unchecked. (The absent Operations-side gate is a missing control, not a root cause.)

Description.evidence: Same-day value is at risk when a request arrives close to the 14:00 cut-off and still needs Treasury confirmation.

Impact.evidence: If funding is unavailable the release is deferred and rescheduled to the next available value date.

Root cause.evidence: SME provided two specific causes: (1) Treasury confirmation is manual with no pre-agreed response-time SLA; (2) no fast-track or priority queue for large-value items arriving in the afternoon window — they queue behind normal-priority work. Prior draft rejected as factually wrong (incorrectly claimed no escalation mechanism exists).

content.title: Cut-Off Risk with Treasury Confirmation
content.description: When a large-value release request (EUR 5,000,000 or above) arrives close to the 14:00 CET cut-off and Treasury confirmation of funding availability has not yet been obtained, same-day value is at risk.
content.impact: Same-day value is lost and the release is deferred and rescheduled to the next available value date.
content.rootCause: Treasury funding confirmation is a manual step with no pre-agreed response-time SLA. Large-value items (EUR 5,000,000+) arriving in the afternoon window have no fast-track or priority queue — they queue behind normal-priority work and risk missing same-day value before the 14:00 CET cut-off.

content.title: Structured Pre-Submission Validation Gate
content.theIdea: Introduce a front-office validation gate — embedded in the client portal or ERP connector — that enforces structured beneficiary data, confirms the facility reference is active in FMS, and validates the requested amount against available undrawn limit before the drawdown request enters PWT. Non-conforming submissions are rejected at source with a structured error.
content.expectedBenefit: Eliminates PP-FRDT-001's incomplete-submission problem at source; Operations receives only complete, pre-validated requests; reduces queue pollution and manual repair work during peak processing windows.
content.feasibility: Requires FMS API for real-time headroom lookup and ISO 20022 message validation at submission; moderately complex but well-precedented (Nordea mandatory validation March 2025). Main risk is front-office change-management and ERP integration scope.

The idea.evidence: AI-drafted based on real-time intraday liquidity trend (@trend-5), Deutsche Bank ICR launch (@eu-3), Kyriba TAI (@ft-1) and HSBC 24/7 liquidity platform (@gl-4) — 2026-06-05

content.title: Real-Time Treasury API Liquidity Confirmation
content.theIdea: Replace the manual Treasury confirmation step (PS-FRDT-004) with an API call to the Treasury/Liquidity Platform (SYS-FRDT-004) that returns real-time intraday funding availability for the value date. Eligible drawdowns below a residual manual-override threshold are cleared automatically; only edge cases (funding unavailable, novel positions) route to a Treasury analyst.
content.expectedBenefit: Removes the human-in-the-loop bottleneck at PS-FRDT-004 for standard drawdowns, eliminating the cut-off risk and SLA gap that drive PP-FRDT-002 — especially for large-value items where manual sign-off is both most critical and most time-sensitive.
content.feasibility: Depends on SYS-FRDT-004 exposing a real-time API; Deutsche Bank ICR and Kyriba TAI demonstrate the pattern is proven. High complexity: Treasury Platform API buildout is typically a multi-team change; regulatory review of automation scope required.

The idea.evidence: AI-drafted based on PG-FRDT-001 gap (STP turnaround not tracked) and Pelican AI STP measurement precedent (@ft-4) — 2026-06-05

Expected benefit.evidence: Derived from PG-FRDT-001: the 2-hour STP target is monitored only anecdotally; no actuals tracking exists today — 2026-06-05

Feasibility.evidence: Low complexity: requires timestamping of key PWT events (receipt, first-line approval, execute) and a reporting query; no external dependencies — 2026-06-05

content.title: STP Rate Dashboard and Turnaround SLA Tracking
content.theIdea: Instrument the Payments Workflow Tool to capture timestamps at each step boundary (queue-entry, first-line approval, execute, close) and compute the STP turnaround versus the 2-hour internal target for every drawdown. Expose a live Operations dashboard and daily management report showing STP rate, average turnaround, and exceptions-by-reason.
content.expectedBenefit: Converts PG-FRDT-001's anecdotal monitoring into a fact base; enables Operations to identify queue-delay patterns, report SLA compliance objectively, and build the evidence base needed to justify investment in further automation.
content.feasibility: Low complexity: timestamping is a PWT configuration change; reporting layer is a dashboard query over existing data. No external system dependency. Primary risk is data-quality if PWT events are not consistently recorded today.

The idea.evidence: AI-drafted based on agentic AI operations trend (@trend-1), Pelican AI priority routing (@ft-4) and JPMorgan agentic routing (@gl-1) — 2026-06-05

content.title: Value-Tiered Priority Queue for Large-Value and Time-Critical Items

content.expectedBenefit: Reduces cut-off exposure for large-value drawdowns (PP-FRDT-002) by ensuring they are processed before standard items during peak periods; reduces the risk that a late Treasury confirmation causes a same-day settlement failure; improves average STP turnaround for high-value items.
content.feasibility: Low-to-medium complexity — a queue prioritisation rule in PWT with no external dependencies. Main risk: clear priority criteria are needed to prevent front-office gaming of the priority flag.
