content.title: Inline AI-Powered Sanctions Pre-Screening at Submission
content.theIdea: Integrate an AI-based inline sanctions and AML screening API at the point of submission (PS-FRDT-007 / PS-FRDT-001), delivering a sub-second clear/hold verdict for each drawdown request before it enters the approval queue. Clean payments enter the approval chain as STP-pre-screened; only holds route to the compliance analyst queue.
content.expectedBenefit: Eliminates the screening queue as a contributor to end-to-end turnaround time (PG-FRDT-001) for clean payments; reduces analyst load by filtering out false positives before they reach the queue; improves STP rate for standard drawdowns.
content.feasibility: Moderate complexity: requires replacement or augmentation of SYS-FRDT-003 with an inline API screening vendor (e.g. ComplyAdvantage Mesh, deployable in 2–4 weeks). Key dependency: sanctions list refresh latency and regulatory acceptance of automated pre-clearance.

content.title: FMS real-time API absent — blocks submission validator and automated liquidity check
content.theGap: SYS-FRDT-005 (Facility Management System) does not expose a real-time API for facility status and available-limit queries — required by both the submission validator (TD-1) and the automated liquidity confirmation (TD-3).
content.resolution: Commission a real-time facility-status and available-limit API on SYS-FRDT-005; assess whether a platform upgrade or API wrapper is required. TD-1 and TD-3 are both blocked until this is resolved.
content.status: Open; blocking TD-1 and TD-3. IT architect pass required.

content.title: Sanctions engine not integrated at submission — runs post-intake only
content.theGap: SYS-FRDT-003 (Sanctions Screening Engine) is positioned post-intake (PS-FRDT-002), not at the point of submission. Inline pre-screening (TD-2) requires repositioning the engine or layering a new inline API in front of it.
content.resolution: Decide between repositioning SYS-FRDT-003 or deploying a separate inline screening API (e.g. ComplyAdvantage Mesh) as a front-layer, while preserving the existing engine for analyst review of holds.
content.status: Open; blocking TD-2. Architecture decision required.

content.title: Treasury Platform API absent — automated liquidity confirmation blocked
content.theGap: SYS-FRDT-004 (Treasury/Liquidity Platform) does not expose an API for real-time intraday funding availability — the manual Treasury sign-off is the compensating control. TD-3 cannot be delivered until this gap is closed.
content.resolution: Assess SYS-FRDT-004 vendor roadmap for API connectivity; evaluate alternative intraday liquidity platform integration (e.g. Kyriba TAI, Baton Systems). BCBS 248 supervisory expectations (REG-FRDT-005) add regulatory urgency.
content.status: Open; blocking TD-3. Vendor roadmap assessment required.

content.title: PWT step-level timestamps absent — STP dashboard blocked
content.theGap: PWT does not currently capture step-level timestamps at step boundaries — the root cause of PG-FRDT-001 (anecdotal STP monitoring) and a prerequisite for the STP dashboard (TD-5).
content.resolution: Implement timestamp capture in PWT at step boundaries (queue-entry, first-line approval, execute, close). Assess whether PWT natively supports event logging or requires a configuration change. Lowest-effort gap.
content.status: Open; blocking TD-5. PWT configuration change only.

content.title: Front Office — drawdown request origination
content.theDependency: The Front Office submits drawdown requests that initiate the funds-release process; in the target state they will also interact with the structured submission validation gate.
content.whatCrossesTheBoundary: Drawdown request (facility ID, amount, currency, value date, beneficiary details); in the target state, a structured ISO 20022-aligned submission pre-validated against FMS facility status and VoP beneficiary verification.
content.whyItMatters: Front-office submission quality is the root cause of PP-FRDT-001; the target state requires the front office to adopt the submission validator — a change-management dependency outside Operations' direct control.

content.title: Core Banking System — downstream settlement execution and confirmation
content.theDependency: The Core Banking System executes the actual fund posting and returns a settlement confirmation; the process is complete only when CBS confirms the debit/credit.
content.whatCrossesTheBoundary: Payment instruction from PWT to CBS; posting confirmation (debit/credit) returned to PWT for Confirm and Close; CBS audit trail entries from the settlement leg.
content.whyItMatters: CBS posting is the point of no return; CBS availability and settlement cut-off times are hard external constraints on M-FRDT-003 compliance and PSD2 Art. 83 execution timelines.

content.title: SYS-FRDT-004 can expose a real-time intraday liquidity API
content.theAssumption: SYS-FRDT-004 (Treasury/Liquidity Platform) can be extended to expose a real-time intraday liquidity API within the target delivery timeline, or a compatible third-party integration layer can be deployed in its place.
content.whyItIsUnconfirmed: No vendor API roadmap has been reviewed; the platform's current connectivity is undocumented in the wiki and the existing control (CP-FRDT-004) assumes manual compensation only.
content.impactIfWrong: TD-3 (automated liquidity confirmation) is blocked; PP-FRDT-002 cut-off risk cannot be automated away and TS-2 depends entirely on this assumption.

content.title: Automated pre-clearance acceptable to Compliance and supervisors without formal non-objection
content.theAssumption: Automated inline sanctions pre-clearance (TD-2) and automated Treasury liquidity confirmation (TD-3) are acceptable to the bank's Compliance function and supervisors without a formal supervisory non-objection.
content.whyItIsUnconfirmed: REG-FRDT-001 (AML/sanctions) and REG-FRDT-005 (BCBS 248) set material requirements these automations must satisfy; whether automated pre-clearance is sufficient has not been validated with Compliance or the supervisory authority.
content.impactIfWrong: TD-2 and TD-3 would require formal Compliance sign-off and potentially a supervisory notification period, extending the delivery timeline by 3–6 months.

content.title: Validated, Pre-Screened Intake
content.targetDescription: Drawdown requests enter Operations already complete and pre-screened. A structured validation gate enforces field completeness, confirms facility status via FMS API, and verifies beneficiary details before the request is accepted into PWT. An inline AI sanctions API delivers a clear/hold verdict at submission; clean payments enter the approval queue as pre-screened and only genuine hits route to the compliance analyst queue.
content.whatChanges:
- Receive Request and Validate Request shift from manual inspection and repair to automated gate-passing
- Compliance Screening handles only genuine holds; routine items no longer queue for screening
- Operations Analysts stop manually repairing incomplete submissions
content.rationale: PP-FRDT-001 (the most common delay cause) is eliminated at source; inline screening removes compliance-queue contribution from clean-payment STP turnaround, together raising STP rate and freeing Operations and Compliance capacity.

content.title: Automated Liquidity Confirmation and Priority Routing
content.targetDescription: The manual Treasury funding confirmation for drawdowns ≥EUR 5m is replaced by an automated API call to the Treasury/Liquidity Platform that returns real-time intraday funding availability for the value date. A configurable priority tier in PWT surfaces large-value and time-critical items ahead of standard work. Together these protect same-day value for high-value drawdowns and eliminate the root causes of PP-FRDT-002.
content.whatChanges:
- Liquidity Confirmation (PS-FRDT-004) becomes an automated system check for eligible drawdowns; Treasury analysts handle only edge cases
- Large-value items no longer queue behind standard-priority work
- PP-FRDT-002 cut-off risk is structurally removed for ≥EUR 5m drawdowns
content.rationale: Removes PP-FRDT-002's two root causes — manual Treasury confirmation with no SLA and no fast-track — protecting same-day value and supporting PSD2 Art. 83 / M-FRDT-003 execution compliance.

content.title: STP Observability and Performance Management
content.targetDescription: Every step boundary in PWT captures a timestamp; a live Operations dashboard reports STP rate, average turnaround versus the 2-hour target, and exceptions-by-reason. Daily management reports replace anecdotal monitoring with auditable data. The dashboard becomes the primary signal for directing further automation investment and evidencing SLA compliance.
content.whatChanges:
- PG-FRDT-001's anecdotal STP monitoring is replaced by an automated measurement and reporting layer
- Operations and Management gain real-time performance data on STP rate and turnaround
- The 2-hour STP target becomes objectively reportable; the evidence base for transformation ROI is established
content.rationale: Closes PG-FRDT-001; replaces informal STP monitoring with an auditable data feed; provides the measurement baseline that all subsequent automation decisions depend on to demonstrate value.

content.title: Deploy structured front-office submission validator
content.theDecision: Introduce a submission validation component — embedded in the front-office portal or ERP connector — that enforces mandatory fields, confirms facility status via FMS API and validates beneficiary details via Verification of Payee before the drawdown request enters PWT.
content.optionsConsidered:
- Option A: Extend the front-office portal with a built-in validation layer backed by FMS and VoP APIs
- Option B: Procure a standalone pre-validation service (e.g. SWIFT Pre-Validation, SurePay VoP) integrated at the intake boundary
- Key constraint: SYS-FRDT-005 must expose a real-time facility-status API for either option (see VG-FRDT-001)
content.rationale: PP-FRDT-001 (P1 HIGH, most common delay cause) is best fixed at source. Nordea (CEU-FRDT-004) and Finastra Loan IQ Nexus (CFT-FRDT-003) both demonstrate mandatory validation gates at production scale.

content.title: Integrate inline AI sanctions screening at submission
content.theDecision: Integrate an AI-based inline sanctions and AML screening API at submission, delivering a sub-second clear/hold verdict per drawdown before it enters the approval queue — routing only genuine holds to the compliance analyst queue.
content.optionsConsidered:
- Option A: Replace SYS-FRDT-003 with an inline API-first screening vendor (e.g. ComplyAdvantage Mesh)
- Option B: Layer an inline screening API in front of SYS-FRDT-003, preserving the existing engine for analyst review of holds
- Key constraint: regulatory acceptance of automated pre-clearance must be confirmed before proceeding (see ASM-FRDT-002)
content.rationale: Compliance screening queue time contributes directly to STP turnaround (PG-FRDT-001); ComplyAdvantage Mesh (CFT-FRDT-002) deploys via REST API in 2–4 weeks and Goldman Sachs autonomous AML agents (CGL-FRDT-002) are in production at scale.

content.title: Replace manual Treasury confirmation with real-time API integration
content.theDecision: Commission an API integration between PWT and SYS-FRDT-004 to automate funding-availability confirmation for drawdowns ≥EUR 5m, eliminating manual Treasury sign-off for eligible drawdowns and preserving a manual-override path for edge cases.
content.optionsConsidered:
- Option A: Expose a real-time intraday liquidity API on SYS-FRDT-004 (platform upgrade)
- Option B: Integrate a third-party intraday liquidity platform (e.g. Kyriba TAI, Baton Systems) providing the API layer
- Key constraint: both options require SYS-FRDT-004 to surface real-time position data (see VG-FRDT-003 and ASM-FRDT-001)
content.rationale: PP-FRDT-002's root cause is manual confirmation with no SLA; Deutsche Bank ICR (CEU-FRDT-003), HSBC 24/7 liquidity (CGL-FRDT-004) and Kyriba TAI (CFT-FRDT-001) all demonstrate automated API confirmation at scale. BCBS 248 (REG-FRDT-005) reinforces the regulatory direction.

content.title: Implement value-tiered priority queue in PWT
content.theDecision: Add a configurable priority tier to the PWT queue that surfaces large-value (≥EUR 5m) and time-critical items ahead of standard work, with priority criteria documented and reviewed quarterly.
content.optionsConsidered:
- Option A: Configure priority routing within the existing PWT (lower effort, preferred if PWT supports configurable queue tiers)
- Option B: Replace PWT with a platform supporting native SLA-based routing (may be required if PWT architecture is not configurable)
- Key design decision: who can flag an item as time-critical and how gaming is prevented must be documented
content.rationale: SYS-FRDT-001 has no priority ordering today; large-value items near cut-off risk same-day value loss (PP-FRDT-002). This is the lowest-complexity near-term fix available ahead of the full TD-3 API buildout.

content.title: Instrument PWT with step-level timestamps and STP dashboard
content.theDecision: Implement step-level timestamp capture in PWT at each step boundary and build an Operations dashboard and daily report exposing STP turnaround versus the 2-hour target and exceptions-by-reason.
content.optionsConsidered:
- Option A: Extend existing PWT reporting capabilities (fastest if PWT natively supports event logging)
- Option B: Integrate a process analytics layer (e.g. Pelican AI STP monitoring) for richer analytics and self-learning improvement
- Key constraint: both options require consistent step-boundary events in PWT (see VG-FRDT-004)
content.rationale: PG-FRDT-001 is open — STP compliance is anecdotal. The dashboard is the lowest-effort, earliest-delivery item and a prerequisite for demonstrating STP improvement from all other transformation decisions. Pelican AI (CFT-FRDT-004) demonstrates the measurement model.

content.title: Submission validator: zero false-negative rejections, verified by shadow run
content.requirement: The submission validator must enforce all mandatory fields of the current Validate Request step with zero false-negative rejections of valid submissions, verified by shadow run against 30 days of historical submissions before production rollout.
content.rationale: TD-1 must not block legitimate drawdown requests; completeness verification is the acceptance bar before the gate goes live.
content.acceptanceCriteria:
- Shadow run on ≥30 days of historical submissions shows zero false-negative rejections of submissions accepted by the manual step
- Mandatory field coverage ≥100% of all fields in the PS-FRDT-001 validation checklist

content.title: Inline sanctions screening: 1-second p99 latency, false-positive rate ≤ baseline
content.requirement: Inline sanctions screening must return a clear/hold verdict within 1 second (p99) at peak volume, with a false-positive rate not exceeding the current SYS-FRDT-003 baseline.
content.rationale: TD-2 sits in the critical STP path; latency increase or elevated false-positive rate would worsen the turnaround problem it is designed to solve.
content.acceptanceCriteria:
- p99 verdict latency ≤1 second under peak load
- False-positive rate ≤ current SYS-FRDT-003 benchmark measured over 30-day pilot; no increase in analyst queue depth versus pre-implementation baseline

content.title: Treasury API: 5-second response time, ≥80% automation rate, manual-override path
content.requirement: The Treasury API must return a funding-availability verdict within 5 seconds; the automated path must apply to ≥80% of drawdowns ≥EUR 5m, with a documented manual-override path for edge cases.
content.rationale: TD-3 must reliably clear eligible large-value drawdowns before the 14:00 CET cut-off; the 5-second threshold and 80% automation rate are the minimum to materially reduce PP-FRDT-002 exposure.
content.acceptanceCriteria:
- p99 API response time ≤5 seconds; ≥80% of drawdowns ≥EUR 5m auto-confirmed in first 90 days post-launch
- Manual-override path tested, documented and approved by Treasury

content.title: Priority queue controlled by Ops Team Lead; STP dashboard refreshes within 5 minutes
content.requirement: Priority queue configuration must be controlled by the Operations Team Lead with criteria documented and reviewed quarterly; the STP dashboard must refresh within 5 minutes of posting events with timestamp completeness ≥99.9%.
content.rationale: TD-4 and TD-5 are the earliest-delivery items; configuration control and data quality are the acceptance bar for Operations and the measurement baseline for transformation ROI.
content.acceptanceCriteria:
- Priority criteria approved by Operations Team Lead and reviewed at quarter-end
- STP dashboard refresh latency ≤5 minutes over 7-day measurement period; timestamp completeness ≥99.9% over 30 days

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

What the client does.evidence: drawdown submission — front-office submits drawdown via the online lending portal / API (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Drawdown Submission
content.whatTheClientDoes: The front office completes the drawdown request form in the online lending portal or via API, populating facility ID, amount, currency, value date, and beneficiary details, then submits to the Operations queue.
content.whatTheBankDoes: The Payments Workflow Tool queues the request, assigns a workflow ID, and timestamps receipt.
content.experience: Clear and structured for experienced users; the portal provides field guidance. First-time or infrequent submitters may overlook mandatory document attachments, setting up a return at the next step.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

What the client does.evidence: 'item returned — incomplete' notification — front office must correct and resubmit (Markus Holzhäuser)

Experience.evidence: the moment a request is returned as incomplete (trust dip) — Markus Holzhäuser

content.title: Incomplete-Request Return Notification
content.whatTheClientDoes: The front office receives the return notification, reads the stated reason for rejection, corrects the missing fields or documents, and resubmits the request via the portal.
content.whatTheBankDoes: The Operations Analyst sends a return notification via email or secure message identifying the missing field or document and referencing the original workflow ID.
content.experience: A trust dip — the front office discovers only after submission that their request was incomplete. There is no pre-submission check to prevent it, and a full resubmission is required to proceed.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

What the client does.evidence: Treasury-confirmation wait — front office has no status visibility while item sits in queue (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Treasury-Confirmation Wait
content.whatTheClientDoes: The front office waits with no system-visible queue position. The RM contacts Operations by phone or email to ask for a status update and relays whatever was learned to the corporate client.
content.whatTheBankDoes: Treasury manually checks the liquidity platform and records the funding confirmation in the Payments Workflow Tool, with no SLA and no proactive status notification to the front office.
content.experience: Opaque and passive — the front office has no indicator of queue position or expected response time. Uncertainty is highest on large-value items near the 14:00 CET cut-off, where missing the window has real commercial cost.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

What the client does.evidence: beneficiary callback-verification call — phone callback line for manual-release beneficiary verification (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Beneficiary Callback-Verification Call
content.whatTheClientDoes: A named beneficiary contact waits to be reached by phone by an Operations Analyst, then verbally confirms the account details required for the manual release.
content.whatTheBankDoes: An Operations Analyst places an outbound call to the named contact to verbally verify beneficiary account details before proceeding with the manual release.
content.experience: Interruptive and time-sensitive — the release is stalled until the named contact is reached. If unavailable, the Analyst must retry with no alternative route, and neither side can predict when the release will proceed.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

What the client does.evidence: release-complete confirmation to front office (Markus Holzhäuser)

Experience.evidence: the release-complete confirmation arriving in time for same-day value — Markus Holzhäuser

content.title: Release-Complete Confirmation
content.whatTheClientDoes: The front office receives the release confirmation from the Payments Workflow Tool and communicates the outcome — value date and reference details — to the corporate client.
content.whatTheBankDoes: The Operations Analyst sends the confirmation notification via the Payments Workflow Tool, closes the workflow item, and locks the audit log.
content.experience: Positive and conclusive when it arrives in time for same-day value. The experience turns neutral to negative when confirmation arrives after the cut-off and same-day value is lost.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

Description.evidence: Payments Workflow Tool intake — front-office submits drawdown via the online lending portal / API (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Payments Workflow Tool
content.description: The bank's online lending portal and API through which the front office submits drawdown requests and receives structured release confirmations.
content.usage: Used for every drawdown submission and every release-complete confirmation. The primary intake and close-out channel — all structured instructions and outbound confirmations flow through it.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

Description.evidence: email/secure message for returned-item queries (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Email / Secure Messaging
content.description: The bank's secure messaging and email channel used for notifications and follow-up queries on returned or stalled items.
content.usage: Used when Operations returns an incomplete submission to the front office with a reason note, and when the front office queries the status of a returned or stalled item. Lower friction than a phone call; asynchronous.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

Description.evidence: phone callback line for manual-release beneficiary verification (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Phone Callback Line
content.description: A direct phone line used by Operations for outbound beneficiary verification calls on manual-release items.
content.usage: Invoked on manual-release items requiring beneficiary verification by phone call to a named contact. Also used when the front office chases status on items with no queue visibility.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

Description.evidence: the front-office relationship manager relay for status (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Front-Office RM Relay
content.description: The front-office Relationship Manager serves as a human relay, passing status updates from Operations to the corporate client during processing.
content.usage: Engaged when the front office has no direct system-facing status visibility and needs to relay updates to the corporate client. The RM contacts Operations informally and passes back whatever status can be obtained.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

The moment.evidence: the release-complete confirmation arriving in time for same-day value (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Release-Complete Confirmation Meets Same-Day Cut-Off
content.theMoment: The release-complete confirmation lands in the front office before the settlement cut-off, confirming same-day value for the corporate client.
content.whyItMatters: Same-day value is the core delivery promise. When the confirmation arrives in time, the bank has met its commitment. A day's delay on a corporate drawdown can carry real cost — working capital impact, covenant compliance, or a broken counterparty obligation.
content.designImplication: Protect the time window from submission to confirmation. Any step without an SLA or without a fast-track path — notably Treasury confirmation — is a structural risk. The process should surface expected confirmation times to the front office proactively, before the cut-off.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

The moment.evidence: the moment a request is returned as incomplete (trust dip) — Markus Holzhäuser

content.title: Request Returned as Incomplete — Trust Dip
content.theMoment: The front office receives a return notification stating that their submitted drawdown request was rejected as incomplete and must be corrected and resubmitted.
content.whyItMatters: This is the most common failure mode in the process. Each return erodes confidence in the submission channel. Repeated occurrences train the front office to distrust the portal or to over-communicate before submitting, adding noise to the process.
content.designImplication: Pre-submission validation in the origination tool would eliminate most returns before they happen. At minimum, return notifications must be specific enough that the front office can correct and resubmit without a follow-up call.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

The moment.evidence: the near-cut-off Treasury confirmation for a large-value item — does it clear before 14:00 CET (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Near-Cut-Off Treasury Confirmation on a Large-Value Item
content.theMoment: A large-value drawdown (EUR 5,000,000 or above) sits in Liquidity Confirmation in the afternoon, and the front office does not know whether Treasury will confirm before the 14:00 CET cut-off.
content.whyItMatters: Stakes are high in both directions: confirmation means same-day release; a miss means a full day's delay on a material drawdown. The front office is in an information vacuum — no queue position, no expected response time, no fast-track path.
content.designImplication: Large-value items near the cut-off require a dedicated fast-track signal. A minimum viable fix is a priority flag for afternoon items above the threshold, combined with a proactive Treasury status call to the front office before the cut-off is reached.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

Description.evidence: MANUAL-RELEASE CALLBACK VERIFICATION — the beneficiary callback is slow, depends on reaching a named contact, and stalls the release (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Manual-Release Callback Verification
content.description: On manual-release items, Operations must place an outbound phone call to a named beneficiary contact to verify account details before the release can proceed. There is no alternative verification route and no SLA on reaching the contact.
content.rootCause: The manual-release path has no digital verification mechanism. Beneficiary verification relies on a phone call to a named individual — if that individual is unavailable, the release stalls with no fallback.
content.clientImpact: The front office and corporate client experience an unpredictable delay on every manual-release item. Total stall time depends entirely on when Operations reaches the named contact — potentially hours — with no visibility into the wait.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

Description.evidence: NEAR-CUT-OFF TREASURY CONFIRMATION — large-value items arriving in the afternoon have no fast-track and risk missing 14:00 CET (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Near-Cut-Off Treasury Confirmation
content.description: Large-value items requiring Treasury confirmation that arrive in the afternoon have no fast-track path and risk missing the 14:00 CET cut-off, causing a same-day value failure for the corporate client.
content.rootCause: Treasury confirmation is a manual step with no response-time SLA. There is no priority queue or fast-track mechanism for high-value items arriving in the afternoon — they queue behind normal-priority work.
content.clientImpact: Same-day value is lost and the release defers to the next business day. For a corporate client this can mean a missed working-capital window, a broken commitment to a counterparty, or a covenant compliance issue on the drawdown date.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

Description.evidence: incomplete-submission returns forcing a full resubmission round-trip (Markus Holzhäuser)

content.title: Incomplete-Submission Return Round-Trip
content.description: When a drawdown submission is returned as incomplete, the front office must correct the identified deficiency and resubmit the full request. There is no partial-correction or save-and-resume mechanism in the portal.
content.rootCause: No pre-submission validation exists in the front-office origination tool to catch missing fields or inactive facility IDs before the request enters the Operations queue.
content.clientImpact: Cycle time extends by the full correction-and-resubmission round-trip, which may push the item past same-day STP targets. The friction cost falls entirely on the client side, and the return gives no advance warning.

meta.source: Markus Holzhäuser, Domain Architect — FRDT-001 client-journey session 2026-06-05

Description.evidence: no status visibility for the front office while an item sits in the queue (they chase by phone/email) — Markus Holzhäuser

content.title: No Status Visibility While in Queue
content.description: Once a drawdown request is submitted, the front office has no real-time visibility into its queue position, current processing stage, or expected completion time.
content.rootCause: The Payments Workflow Tool provides no client-facing or front-office-facing status tracker. The only route to a status update is to contact the RM, who must then contact Operations directly.
content.clientImpact: The front office is forced to chase status via phone or email, creating coordination overhead on both sides. Uncertainty is worst for large-value items near the cut-off, where the front office cannot plan client communication without knowing whether same-day value will be met.

TR-FRDT-001.author: admin
TR-FRDT-001.text: Is ISO 20022 CBPR+ actually in scope for THIS funds-release process, or does it only bite at the downstream cross-border settlement leg we explicitly scoped out? If it's only relevant to the SWIFT leg, this trend may be a disregard for our internal release desk — challenge the relevance before we build target work on it.

CP-FRDT-002.author: admin
CP-FRDT-002.text: This control's activity says clean items "pass automatically" — but we never documented the false-positive rate or who reviews the auto-pass logic. An auditor will ask: what assurance is there that the screening engine's auto-clear threshold is calibrated and periodically validated? Suggest adding a periodic model-validation / threshold-review obligation to the control activity.

CP-FRDT-002.author: Control & Compliance Specialist
CP-FRDT-002.text:
**Comment-review summary — CP-FRDT-002 (Sanctions and AML Screening)**

**Incorporated (1):** The open comment flagged that the control activity was silent on engine quality assurance — no documented threshold-calibration obligation or periodic validation. The 'Control activity' block was rewritten (to fit the 50-word schema limit) to preserve all original facts and add: "Thresholds and matching rules are validated at least annually by Compliance: calibration, FP/FN sampling, watchlist-update confirmation."

**Declined (0):** None.

**Left open (0):** None.

**Linked elements to check:** PS-FRDT-002 ('Compliance Screening' process step) describes the same operational step and does not mention the periodic validation obligation — it may now be understated and should be reviewed for consistency with the updated control activity.

Finding.evidence: SME described: '4-eyes occasionally bypassed under cut-off time pressure' (HIGH severity) as a realistic historical audit finding for a funds-release desk (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: 4-Eyes SoD Occasionally Bypassed Under Cut-Off Pressure
content.finding: Internal audit observed instances where a single Operations Analyst completed both first-line and second-line approval records under cut-off time pressure, bypassing the 4-eyes SoD requirement. In each observed case the Payments Workflow Tool's enforcement block was circumvented via a shared team login that had not been individually deactivated.
content.recommendation: Deactivate all shared team logins and enforce individual user IDs with PWT role-based access controls. Add automated alerting to flag any approval sequence where both records are created within two minutes — indicative of single-person dual entry.

Finding.evidence: SME described: 'incomplete sanctions-screening audit trail on manually-adjudicated hits' (MEDIUM severity) as a realistic historical audit finding (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: Incomplete Sanctions-Screening Audit Trail on Manually Adjudicated Hits
content.finding: A regulatory review found that the audit trail for sanctions-screening items escalated for manual adjudication was incomplete: decisions lacked a documented rationale, timestamp, and named adjudicator in approximately 30 percent of reviewed cases. Where a SAR was filed, the link between the screening outcome and the SAR reference was not captured in the Payments Workflow Tool.
content.recommendation: Mandate that Compliance records a structured adjudication note — rationale, decision, adjudicator name, and (where applicable) SAR reference — in the Payments Workflow Tool before an item can be cleared or frozen. Add this as a mandatory field to the PWT Compliance adjudication screen.

Finding.evidence: SME described: 'no evidence of periodic facility-limit reconciliation' (MEDIUM severity) as a realistic historical audit finding (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: No Evidence of Periodic Facility-Limit Reconciliation
content.finding: Internal audit found no evidence that the per-transaction facility limit check (CP-FRDT-001) is complemented by a periodic reconciliation of the Facility Management System's available-limit register against actual outstanding drawn balances. Without this, FMS data drift — stale or incorrectly decremented limits — would not be detected and could allow a release to proceed on an incorrect limit.
content.recommendation: Establish a weekly automated reconciliation of the FMS available-limit register against Core Banking System drawn balances. Any discrepancy above a materiality threshold should be escalated to Credit Risk before the next release cycle. Amend CP-FRDT-001 to reference the reconciliation cadence.

Description.evidence: SME listed 'incomplete/invalid request' as one of at least 5 exceptions, raised at PS-FRDT-001 Validate Request (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: Incomplete or Invalid Request
content.description: A drawdown request fails the completeness or eligibility check at the Validate Request step — missing required fields, an inactive or incorrect facility ID, an invalid value date, or absent supporting documents such as the signed drawdown notice or facility utilisation request.
content.handling: The Operations Analyst returns the item to the front office via the Payments Workflow Tool with a specific reason code identifying the deficiency. The item retains its original workflow ID and re-enters the Validate Request step upon resubmission. Cycle time is measured from original receipt, not the resubmission date.
content.impact: Cycle time extends by the correction-and-resubmission round-trip, typically 30 minutes to several hours; repeated returns risk missing the same-day STP target and erode front-office confidence.

Description.evidence: SME listed 'confirmed sanctions/AML hit' as one of at least 5 exceptions, raised at PS-FRDT-002 Compliance Screening (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: Confirmed Sanctions or AML Hit
content.description: The Sanctions Screening Engine generates a confirmed match against a sanctions list or AML watchlist during the Compliance Screening step, indicating that the beneficiary, payer, or payment context is prohibited or subject to an enhanced-due-diligence hold.
content.handling: The item is frozen immediately and cannot proceed. Compliance takes ownership: they investigate the hit, determine whether a Suspicious Activity Report must be filed, and open a formal case in the AML case-management system. The release remains blocked until Compliance either clears the hit or formally escalates. Operations has no authority to override a confirmed hit.
content.impact: The release is indefinitely suspended; if confirmed as a true match, funds are frozen and the case enters the bank's formal investigation process, with potential regulatory reporting obligations and reputational risk.

Description.evidence: SME listed 'insufficient funding for value date' as one of at least 5 exceptions, raised at PS-FRDT-004 Liquidity Confirmation (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: Insufficient Funding for Value Date
content.description: Treasury confirms that sufficient intraday funding is not available for the requested value date during the Liquidity Confirmation step, preventing the release from proceeding on its originally scheduled date.
content.handling: Treasury records the unavailability in the Payments Workflow Tool and the release is rescheduled to the next available value date with confirmed funding. The front office is notified of the deferral and the revised value date. The item is held in the queue pending rescheduled processing; it does not re-enter the full approval chain unless the instruction changes.
content.impact: Same-day value is lost; the corporate client receives the drawdown on a later date, potentially affecting working-capital planning or commitment fulfilment. The item incurs additional processing overhead.

Description.evidence: SME listed '4-eyes approver unavailable / SoD breach' as one of at least 5 exceptions, raised at PS-FRDT-005 Second-Line Approval (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: 4-Eyes Approver Unavailable or SoD Breach
content.description: No qualified second-line approver is available to complete second-line authorisation at the Second-Line Approval step, or the Payments Workflow Tool blocks approval because the only available approver is the same individual who performed first-line approval.
content.handling: The item is held pending an available approver. The Operations Team Lead is escalated to source one or to grant a documented time-limited exception. The item may not progress without second-line authorisation from a different individual; the system blocks the same user ID from completing both approval steps.
content.impact: The release is delayed until a qualified approver is available, extending cycle time and risking same-day STP targets. Improper resolution without documentation creates an audit trail deficiency and regulatory exposure.

Description.evidence: SME listed 'missed same-day cut-off' as one of at least 5 exceptions, raised at PS-FRDT-008 Execute Release (Markus Holzhäuser, 2026-06-05)

content.title: Missed Same-Day Cut-Off
content.description: A release item completes all approval steps but reaches the Execute Release step after the applicable same-day settlement cut-off — 14:00 CET for EUR or earlier for other currencies — making same-day value execution impossible.
content.handling: The Operations Analyst records the cut-off miss in the Payments Workflow Tool and notifies the front office. The item is held for next-business-day execution; the value date is updated accordingly. If the front office wishes to cancel, a separate cancellation instruction is required.
content.impact: Same-day value is lost; the corporate client receives funds on the next business day, potentially breaching the bank's PSD2 Art. 83 D+1 execution obligation if the delay causes an over-D+1 delivery.

content.title: PSD2 Execution Timeline — No Dedicated Monitoring Control
content.theGap: No dedicated control monitors aggregate cycle time across the end-to-end release process to enforce PSD2 Art. 83's D+1 execution requirement. Compliance with the timeline is assumed rather than actively tracked.
content.risk: An undetected breach of the D+1 execution obligation — a release that executes on D+2 or later — constitutes a PSD2 non-compliance event reportable to the supervisory authority.
content.remediation: Implement an automated SLA clock in the Payments Workflow Tool that measures receipt-to-execution cycle time and alerts Operations and Compliance when any item approaches the D+1 limit, enabling proactive intervention before a breach.

content.title: No Automated Facility-Limit Reconciliation
content.theGap: The per-transaction Facility Limit Check (CP-FRDT-001) verifies available limit at point of submission, but no periodic reconciliation of the Facility Management System's limit register against actual outstanding drawn balances exists to detect data drift between transactions.
content.risk: FMS limit-register drift — arising from manual updates, interface failures, or system errors — could allow releases to proceed on an overstated available limit, creating a credit over-exposure that CP-FRDT-001 would not catch.
content.remediation: Establish a weekly reconciliation of the FMS available-limit register against Core Banking System drawn balances, with automated escalation to Credit Risk for any discrepancy above a defined materiality threshold.
